title: FIN Bridge Common Definitions
description: Shared building blocks ($defs) referenced by the command, result, event, hello, and poll envelopes. Not a standalone message; only its $defs are consumed via $ref.

protocolVersion.title: Protocol Version
protocolVersion.description: Integer wire-contract version, starting at 1. Present in hello and every poll exchange so the server can gate on it and refuse skew with PROTOCOL_VERSION_MISMATCH. Distinct from agentScriptVersion.

agentId.title: Agent Id
agentId.description: Stable identifier the agent chooses for the computer running it. Scopes the command queue, liveness, and event sequence server-side. Not a FIN component UUID.

agentScriptVersion.title: Agent Script Version
agentScriptVersion.description: Semver of the in-world Lua agent script, reported by the agent. Advisory: lets the server log/display which script build is connected. Independent of protocolVersion.

target.title: Command Target
target.description: How a command names the component(s) it acts on. Exactly one addressing mode, mirroring FIN's findComponent query model.

byNick.title: By Nick
byNick.description: Player-assigned nick/group string, resolved agent-side via component.findComponent. Not guaranteed unique; a single-target write against an ambiguous nick yields AMBIGUOUS_TARGET, never a silent first-match.

byId.title: By Id
byId.description: Stable FIN component UUID, resolved via component.proxy. Preferred once discovered, since UUIDs are unambiguous.

byClass.title: By Class
byClass.description: FIN class query (e.g. Build_SmelterMk1_C) for reads and broadcasts that intentionally fan out over a component type.

componentRef.title: Resolved Component Reference
componentRef.description: The resolved form a component takes in discovery results and event sources. Always carries the authoritative UUID; nick/class/displayName are advisory and may be absent.

nick.title: Nick
nick.description: Player-assigned nick, if any. Advisory and not unique.

class.title: Class
class.description: FIN component class name, if known.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: Human-friendly name from the game, if available.

errorCode.title: Error Code
errorCode.description: Typed error code so callers can branch programmatically. New codes are a deliberate, versioned addition to this enum.

errorObject.title: Error Object
errorObject.description: Uniform error shape across result envelopes and HTTP error bodies.

message.title: Error Message
message.description: Human-actionable text. For operator-facing failures it names the real-world remedy (e.g. 'Is the FIN computer powered and running the agent script?').

details.title: Error Details
details.description: Free-form structured context, e.g. the candidate list for AMBIGUOUS_TARGET or version bounds for PROTOCOL_VERSION_MISMATCH.
